title: cystium cicer
intro: Cystium cicer, also known as Cicuta maculata, is a perennial plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as water hemlock. It is one of the most toxic plants in North America, with a long history of medicinal use in traditional herbalism. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anthelmintic, expectorant, and diuretic effects. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion for its medicinal properties, but caution should be exercised due to its high toxicity.
